source.title: EU Foreign Subsidies Regulation Investigation and Customs Reform Impact on Temu

extractions.claim: EC raided Temu's Dublin HQ on December 10, 2025 under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation (FSR)
extractions.evidence: The European Commission confirmed that it 'has carried out an unannounced inspection at the premises of a company active in the e-commerce sector in the EU, under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation.' Reuters reported Temu as the company inspected. This was the second FSR dawn raid ever conducted.

verification.verifier_notes: Confirmed by Irish Times, RTE, Bloomberg, and multiple EU law firm analyses

extractions.claim: FSR targets foreign subsidies including zero-interest loans, below-cost financing, tax breaks, and preferential treatment
extractions.evidence: The FSR came into force on 12 July 2023 as a means to limit potential distortions of competition in the EU internal market as a result of financial contributions from non-EU governments. Foreign subsidies may take the form of zero-interest loans, other below-cost financing, tax breaks, or preferential tax treatment.

verification.verifier_notes: Source: Van Doorne legal analysis of FSR

extractions.claim: EU to impose €3 flat customs fee on parcels under €150 starting July 1, 2026; full elimination of de minimis by 2028
extractions.evidence: The new fee will take effect July 1, 2026, and will apply to all low-value parcels purchased online and shipped from non-EU nations. The fee applies per parcel, not per item. An additional €2 handling fee for platforms planned for November 2026. Full customs duty system via Customs Data Hub launching 2028.

verification.verifier_notes: Source: European Commission December 11 release; Euronews reporting

extractions.claim: 4.6 billion parcels valued under €150 entered the EU in 2024; over 90% from China; 65%+ undervalued or split
extractions.evidence: The European Commission estimates that roughly 4.6 billion parcels valued under €150 entered the EU in 2024, more than 90% of which came from China. Furthermore, more than 65% of those parcels were undervalued or split into multiple packages to stay under the €150 threshold.

extractions.claim: Temu has ~116 million average monthly users in the EU despite launching only in April 2023
extractions.evidence: Temu has drawn in tens of millions of shoppers globally with rock-bottom prices, and already has around 116 million average monthly users in the EU, despite only launching in the market in April 2023.

extractions.claim: France imposed up to €5/item eco-tax in 2025 rising to €10 by 2030, plus €40M Shein fine; Italy fined Shein €1M
extractions.evidence: France imposed up to €5 per item eco-tax in 2025, rising to €10 by 2030, plus ad/influencer bans for ultra-fast fashion, and a €40m fine on Shein for deceptive promotions. Italy fined Shein €1m for misleading environmental claims.

verification.verifier_notes: National-level enforcement actions in EU member states

extractions.claim: New EU Digital Fairness Act and revamped CPC Regulation announced for 2026 targeting dark patterns and addictive design
extractions.evidence: Commissioner McGrath announced two major enforcement initiatives for 2026: a revamped CPC Regulation giving the EU stronger centralised powers to pursue cross-border breaches, and a new Digital Fairness Act to crack down on dark patterns, addictive design features, and unfair personalised advertising.

severity_assessment.action: EU FSR investigation (dawn raid) + customs reform (€150 de minimis elimination)
severity_assessment.rationale: FSR investigation is early-stage (dawn raid only, no formal charges yet). Outcome uncertain but could force disclosure of any Chinese government financial support. Customs reform (€3/parcel fee from July 2026) is manageable but adds cost. Temu already adapting with local EU warehousing, similar to US pivot. National-level fines ($1.7M Poland) are cosmetic. Cumulative pressure across DSA + FSR + customs is the real concern.
severity_assessment.next_milestone: FSR formal investigation decision expected H1-H2 2026; €3 customs fee takes effect July 1, 2026; Digital Fairness Act proposal expected 2026
